item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey you! Yes, you scrolling through recent posts right now. This article has had major revisions since it was first posted, but very few people have read it in this new state! Hell, all the comments above this one are from before the article was revised.

If you like star quality Fifthist articles, come take a peek at this one. If you downvoted on this article before the revisions, I highly encourage you to reread it cause your stance on it may change!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a weird targeted advertisement SCP, I really like this.

That said, I find the fact that it involves eating the phone to be… entirely tacked on? Like, it feels like its just there as another super weird thing it does, and like the ad has nothing to do with that effect.

It just feels really unnescessary to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think what you DO have going for you here (and take this with a grain if salt, as I'm not as familiar with Fifthism as others on this site, since I'm not particularly a fan of that GoI) is you've nailed down one of the unique aspects of Fifthist skips, which is how pervasive and difficult to contain they are.

They are designed to reach as many people as possible to spread their message and they are singularly effective at it compared to other GoIs. Remember that book they wrote?

This, I think, has that for sure. It's an advertisement you can't close, it spreads indiscriminately and there are instances in the wild of people up and disappearing because of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you very much Kaiser! This skip changed a lot during the editing process, and I definitely appreciate you letting me know what works and what doesn't in the final version.

Pondering and plotting...
Ponder no more

The "eating phones" thing came about because I really really want to do an incident crossing over with SCP-3900, and finally solve the mystery of what happens when a person bites a 3900-infected router.

In the process, unfortunately, I made the ad a pretty cliche "thing what makes you crazy to death." The body horror used to be even worse, alas - this version is toned down considerably from early drafts. Yet you and several reviewers (including 3900's author) also felt it was over the top.

I was worried the retargeting parts and technical speak would get boring because I tend to over-explain. But I'm glad it worked, and will definitely try to clarify that a bit when I revise.

I hate to plow under the idea that got me started. But I even more hate to have an article stay crappy and unsatisfying because I couldn't make the idea work.

Again, many thanks. And I will revisit this, hopefully soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Weird, I just tried posting this in my phone glitched out. uh-oh!

I thought it was alright overall. I only needed to ask my web designer wife one or two questions for clarification so overall I think you did a pretty good job explaining some of the nuts and bolts.

The idea of a fifthist web advertisement that propagates to nearby devices on a conversion is pretty cool, but I didn't really understand how eating your phone fit into the grander scheme besides just being really weird, which, granted, is a trademark of fifthists. However it felt a little bit like body horror for the sake of having some body Horror in the skip.

I think the overall idea is a solid one, but the "eating phones" aspect could maybe use some further consideration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, NOW it posts. See below, I posted this twice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
original post Jan 3 2017: Both images are from Wikimedia Commons. I've gotten crits from Lazar Lyusternik, Taylor_Itkin, CaptainKirby, BoogieMan23, Ty_Gently, DrChandra, MaliceAF and Voct. If it sinks in spite of all this, I have none but myself to blame.

Many thanks for reading!

want headcanon?
cease to observe headcanon

This ad was developed as a recruitment tool by a group of Fifthists who successfully migrated to Alpha Centauri in the mid-20th century. They can monitor viewers telepathically when it's being played, but it has to be uploaded to the internet by co-conspirators on earth.

Previous versions unsuccessfully tried to have other GoIs as the ad's creators, but a couple reviewers pointed out why those wouldn't work. The Scientologists Fifthists are both sufficiently good at marketing AND sufficiently callous towards human life to create something with these effects.

EDIT: Since my by-golly-gee first SCP here has been hovering around zero for almost two weeks, it seems I've done some things well and other things poorly. But I'm not entirely sure which bits are which! If you happen to have a clue to spare, I would definitely appreciate the guidance.

Edit Nov 16 2018: Changed the "makes you eat your phone" effect to a "you are sad the judges voted no and want to call them back to pleas your case" effect. Hopefully this is an improvement!
